sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: KVC Westerlo are struggling, with no wins in their last 3 matches and a concerning streak of 5 games without a clean sheet. Cercle Brugge, on the other hand, have shown mixed results, managing a win in their last 5 but conceding first in 6 of their last 7 matches, highlighting defensive vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Recent head-to-head encounters between KVC Westerlo and Cercle Brugge have been competitive, with each team securing 2 wins in their last 5 matchups and a draw in between. Their last meeting saw Cercle Brugge dominate with a 4-1 victory, while both teams have scored in 4 of their last 5 clashes.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: KVC Westerlo, sitting 10th with 24 points, have a strong offensive output with 28 goals scored (4th in the league) but struggle defensively, conceding 30 goals. Cercle Brugge, positioned 15th with 17 points, have also scored 25 goals but concede frequently (30 goals against), indicating a match with potential for goals on both sides.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Westerlo, Nacho Ferri (5 goals, 2 assists) and Arthur Piedfort (high average rating of 7.1) could be pivotal. Cercle Brugge rely on Steve Ngoura (4 goals, 3 assists) and Gary Magnee (leading with 13 big chances created) to spark their attack.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore's prediction of a 49% chance for a home win, coupled with Westerlo's slightly better league standing and home advantage at Het Kuipje, they are the favorites. However, Cercle Brugge's ability to score and their history of high-scoring games against Westerlo suggest a tight contest with goals likely on both ends.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Cercle Brugge leads KVC Westerlo 2-0 in a match played at Het Kuipje. The away side has taken full advantage of their opportunities, with goals coming early and late in the first half. Westerlo, despite dominating possession, has failed to convert their chances into goals, leaving them with a significant deficit to overcome. Cercle Brugge's clinical finishing has been the key difference so far, while Westerlo will need to sharpen their attack in the second half to mount a comeback.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: KVC Westerlo has controlled the ball for the majority of the first half, showing intent with numerous entries into the final third and penalty area. However, their inability to capitalize on big chances and a missed opportunity that hit the woodwork highlight their lack of precision in front of goal. Cercle Brugge, on the other hand, has been far more efficient despite lower possession. Their fewer but more decisive entries into dangerous areas have resulted in two well-taken goals. Defensively, Cercle Brugge has held firm, while Westerlo's backline has struggled to cope with the away side's sharp attacks.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: The first half began with a strong push from Cercle Brugge, who set the tone early with consistent attacking pressure, culminating in their opening goal. Momentum then shifted briefly towards Westerlo as they attempted to respond with a series of attacking spells. However, Cercle Brugge regained control at key moments, striking again with a crucial goal just before the break. Westerlo showed flashes of dominance in the middle stages of the half with sustained periods of attack, but their inability to break through Cercle Brugge's defense was evident. Towards the end, momentum fluctuated, with both teams trading attacking intent, but Cercle Brugge ultimately had the final say.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players and tactical insights
halftimeSections.text: For Cercle Brugge, Adewumi Oluwaseun has been a standout, scoring one of the goals and posing a constant threat in attack. Gary Magnee has also been instrumental with his assist and defensive contributions. On the Westerlo side, players like Allahyar Sayyadmanesh and Dogucan Haspolat have shown creativity in midfield, but their efforts haven't translated into goals. Tactically, Westerlo may need to push more aggressively in the second half, potentially taking risks at the back to close the gap. Cercle Brugge might look to sit deeper and exploit counter-attacking opportunities, given their lead and efficiency so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: The second half promises to be an intriguing battle as Westerlo will likely come out with renewed energy to overturn the deficit. They have the statistical edge in possession and territorial play, which could translate into goals if they improve their finishing. Cercle Brugge, however, will be confident in their ability to defend their lead and strike on the break. The home side's desperation for goals could leave gaps in defense, which Cercle Brugge might capitalize on. Expect a high-intensity half with Westerlo pushing hard, but Cercle Brugge's clinical edge could prove decisive.
